metadata.greeting: This weekend in Hackney is bursting with fun! Get ready for hands-on activities, outdoor adventures, and maybe a trip to see some animals. With the weather looking great for outdoor activities, there's something for every little explorer.

activities.title: Library Club at the Hackney Showroom
activities.description: Reading corner and diverse book-pairing service.
activities.whyItWorks: Free, drop-in, and caters to a wide age range.

transport.fromHackney: Various bus routes and Overground to Hackney Downs or Hackney Central
transport.tip: Check term times before heading out.

activities.title: Young V&A
activities.description: Interactive galleries designed to encourage children to imagine, play and design.
activities.whyItWorks: Hands-on activities, workshops, and exhibitions specifically designed for children. Has a Mini Museum for under 2s.

transport.fromHackney: Central Line to Bethnal Green
transport.tip: Check for special exhibitions and events.

activities.title: Museum of London Docklands
activities.description: Interactive exhibits about the history of London's waterfront.
activities.whyItWorks: Features Mudlarks, an interactive gallery specifically designed for children. Child-friendly activities and special red signs explaining things in an easy-to-read way.

transport.fromHackney: Jubilee Line to Canary Wharf
transport.tip: The museum is generally not too crowded.

activities.title: Biodiversity Playground
activities.description: Small, beautifully designed wooden playground with a sweet pond theme.
activities.whyItWorks: Perfect for toddlers, and located within the expansive Olympic Park for additional exploration.

transport.fromHackney: Various bus routes or Overground to Stratford
transport.tip: Combine with a walk around the Olympic Park.

activities.title: V&A Museum of Childhood
activities.address: Bethnal Green, Cambridge Heath Rd, London E2 9PA
activities.description: The Young V&A is a vibrant and interactive museum designed specifically for children and families. Formerly known as the Museum of Childhood, this recently renovated playful space puts children at the forefront with hands-on activities, workshops, and exhibitions.
activities.whyItWorks: It's free to enter, fun for all ages, and they have a Mini Museum dedicated to children under 2.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central line to Bethnal Green station.
transport.tip: Check online for special events.

activities.title: Spitalfields City Farm
activities.description: A community farm with a variety of animals.
activities.whyItWorks: Chance to see animals up close. Often has special Easter activities.

transport.fromHackney: Bus routes or Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Check for special Easter activities.

activities.title: Discover Children's Story Centre
activities.description: Two floors of immersive play space known as the Story Worlds as well as the Story Garden playground.
activities.whyItWorks: Immersive play space, with Story Garden playground.

transport.fromHackney: Various bus routes or Overground to Stratford
transport.tip: Check website for opening times.

activities.title: Library Club at the Hackney Showroom
activities.description: Reading corner and diverse book-pairing service.
activities.whyItWorks: Free, drop-in, and caters to a wide age range.

transport.fromHackney: Various bus routes and Overground to Hackney Downs or Hackney Central
transport.tip: Check term times before heading out.

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.description: Explore dinosaurs, fascinating creatures, and intriguing science.
activities.whyItWorks: Dinosaurs gallery, earthquake simulator, and the iconic Hope the blue whale skeleton. Family-friendly activities often available.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to Green Park, then Piccadilly Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Book a free timed entry ticket in advance to avoid queues.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.description: Discover how the world works with interactive exhibits.
activities.whyItWorks: The Garden is an interactive space for younger children. Wonderlab offers hands-on experiences. Free entry, but some exhibits require separate tickets.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to Green Park, then Piccadilly Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Book tickets for Wonderlab in advance.

activities.title: Golders Hill Park Zoo
activities.description: Small zoo with rare species of birds and animals.
activities.whyItWorks: Completely free to enter and features animals like wallabies and lemurs. Also has swimming ponds and a lido in the same area.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Northern Line to Golders Green, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Combine with a picnic in the park.

activities.title: Coram's Fields
activities.description: Seven acres dedicated entirely to children, with an adventure playground, sand pits, a paddling pool (summer), and a small city farm.
activities.whyItWorks: Great place for kids to let off steam with diverse play options. Adults only allowed if accompanying a child, ensuring a safe environment.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to Euston, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Paddling pool is only open in the summer months.

activities.title: British Museum
activities.description: Explore world art and artifacts from various cultures and time periods.
activities.whyItWorks: Egyptian galleries are a highlight for kids. Offers activity backpacks and family trails. Free entry, but booking an entrance ticket in advance is recommended.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to Euston, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Pick up an activity backpack at the family desk.

activities.title: Diana Memorial Playground
activities.description: Large playground with a Peter Pan-inspired pirate ship and surrounding beach, sensory trail, and teepees.
activities.whyItWorks: Enormous pirate ship is a major attraction. Sensory trail adds to the experience. Located in Kensington Gardens for additional exploration.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Central Line to Notting Hill Gate or Bayswater, then a walk through Kensington Gardens.
transport.tip: Can get crowded, especially on weekends.

activities.title: Crystal Palace Dinosaurs
activities.description: Victorian dinosaur sculptures in a park setting.
activities.whyItWorks: Fun for dinosaur lovers. The park also has a maze and lakes.

transport.fromHackney: Overground to Crystal Palace
transport.tip: Combine with a picnic in the park.

activities.title: Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt at Hampton Court Palace
activities.description: Roam around the gardens and find hidden Lindt Gold Bunny statues to win a chocolate treat.
activities.whyItWorks: Fun Easter activity that encourages exploration of the palace gardens.

transport.fromHackney: Train from Liverpool Street to Hampton Court
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Easter Quest at Eltham Palace and Gardens
activities.price: Paid, in addition to paid admission
activities.description: Embark on a fun-filled adventure quest around the house and gardens. Follow the Easter trail, hunt for eggs and answer questions along the way to earn a delicious chocolate reward.
activities.whyItWorks: Fun Easter activity that encourages exploration of the palace and gardens.

transport.fromHackney: Train and bus to Eltham Palace
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Zoonormous Egg Hunt at London Zoo
activities.description: Search high and low for six giant Easter eggs hidden around the grounds. Each egg reveals a secret letter, encouraging curious minds to work together to uncover the code hidden throughout the Zoo.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines a visit to the zoo with an Easter egg hunt.

transport.fromHackney: Tube and bus to London Zoo
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: The Very Hungry Caterpillar Trail at Walthamstow Wetlands
activities.description: Discover a variety of illustrated animals nestled amongst the blooming habitats of the reserve, identify the letter on each one and complete the secret word in the activity booklet.
activities.whyItWorks: Outdoor adventure with familiar characters from The Very Hungry Caterpillar.

transport.fromHackney: Various bus routes or Overground to Walthamstow
transport.tip: Check website for directions and opening times.

activities.title: Family Fun at the Cutty Sark
activities.description: Meet characters from the ship's past, follow a family trail, and join in musical storytelling sessions.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive and engaging way to learn about maritime history.

transport.fromHackney: DLR to Cutty Sark
transport.tip: Check website for special events.

activities.title: Battersea Park
activities.description: Scenic escape with lush gardens, a boating lake, playgrounds, and a mini zoo.
activities.whyItWorks: Good for picnics or just a relaxing walk.

transport.fromHackney: Train to Battersea Park Station
transport.tip: Check website for details.

activities.title: REPLAY: A Limitless Recycled Playground
activities.description: Explore a world of play and invention in a recycled playground for children and their grown-ups.
activities.whyItWorks: Creative playground, good for all ages

transport.fromHackney: Various tube and bus routes
transport.tip: Check website for details.
